title: Ghosts of Atwood
author: Victor Lesniewski
synopsis: Ghosts of Atwood follows Quinn, a young Black cadet newly enrolled at an all-boys military school in rural America, as he navigates the brutal hazing, racial tension, and dark secrets lurking within the institution. Through a blend of dramatized memory and present-day reflection, the play explores how institutional power, peer violence, and silent complicity shape the lives of vulnerable boys left behind by their families. As mysterious "ghosts" prowl the dormitory at night, Quinn must find his footing in a place designed to break spirits and rebuild them in conformity.

title: Good People
author: Victory Lesniewski
synopsis: Margaret, a fifty-year-old working-class woman in South Boston, loses her job at a Dollar Store due to chronic lateness caused by caring for her disabled daughter Joyce. Desperate for employment, she reaches out to an old acquaintance, Dr. Michael Dillon, who has become a successful fertility specialist in a wealthy Boston suburb. As Margaret navigates the job search and reconnects with her past, the play explores themes of class, ambition, and the struggles of working-poor families.

title: maga
author: Sam Abney
synopsis: When cash-strapped Greg Johnson takes a job at a Republican rally, a viral interview launch him into unexpected conservative fame. His friend and roommate Juliet seizes the opportunity to craft him into a young Republican darling as part of an elaborate plan to expose political gullibility. As Greg's influence grows and speaking fees skyrocket, he must grapple with whether his capitalist motives or ideology are driving his controversial statements, blurring the lines between performance and conviction in the age of social media influence.

title: SPLINTERED
author: Emily Aboud
synopsis: SPLINTERED is a cabaret-play hybrid performed by three women exploring queer identity and culture in the Caribbean. Through interconnected vignettes, interviews, and satirical performances, the play examines the experiences of LGBTQ+ women in Trinidad, Jamaica, and the diaspora, blending humor with serious social commentary on colonialism, homophobia, and the liberatory power of Caribbean carnival culture.

title: Eternal Hydra
author: Chris John Abraham
synopsis: Vivian Ezra, a literary scholar, discovers the lost manuscript of "Eternal Hydra" by modernist writer Gordias Carbuncle and attempts to publish it through a major publishing house. Her efforts become complicated when novelist Pauline Newberry publishes a historical novel featuring Carbuncle as a character, and later evidence suggests that Carbuncle may have appropriated material from a Black woman writer named Selma Thomas for one of the novel's chapters. The play explores themes of authorship, literary ownership, and the ethics of literary scholarship.

title: Eleanor & Alice
author: Ellen Abrams
synopsis: Eleanor Roosevelt and her cousin Alice Roosevelt Longworth meet at eight crucial moments spanning their lives, from 1904 to 1962. As two of the most influential women of the 20th century, they navigate their evolving friendship while grappling with marriage, infidelity, political rivalry, and their complex relationship to power and legacy as members of America's most prominent political family.

title: King of Bunker Hill
author: Rochelle Kaplan and Stephen Edelman Abrams
synopsis: Thomas Pope, a ruthless real estate developer, sees his world implode when his fiancée leaves him and he's brutally mugged. Forced to recuperate at his estranged sister's gay-friendly bed and breakfast in Northern California, Thomas initially alienates everyone through his arrogance and insensitivity. As he reluctantly takes over operations while his sister recovers from an accident he caused, Thomas slowly transforms, forming genuine connections with the guests and staff, including a deep friendship with the inn's musician and an unexpected bond with a local bear.

title: That Man Upstairs
author: Rochelle Kaplan Abrams
synopsis: A mechanic named Marty Allen takes a job with room and board at a family garage, gradually winning over the household—but his violent past soon catches up with him when a dangerous ex-convict from his army days begins pursuing him with deadly intent. As the threat escalates, the family finds themselves drawn into a dangerous situation, and shocking revelations about Marty's true identity emerge.

title: Loyalty
author: Rob Ackerman
synopsis: Set during the Harding administration of the early 1920s, "Loyalty" explores the moral corruption that pervades the White House as presidential bodyguard Jim Sloan becomes entangled in a web of scandal involving the President's affair with young Nan Britton, cabinet members enriching themselves through fraudulent dealings, and the various operatives who enable these betrayals. As Sloan grapples with his duty to protect the President against his conscience, the play examines themes of loyalty, ambition, and the price of complicity in a system built on deception.

title: Dear Alien
author: Liz Duffy Adams
synopsis: Dear Alien is a one-person play featuring an advice columnist on the anniversary of her seven-year tenure, struggling with burnout, isolation, and despair. On a day when her rent is overdue and her book deadline looms, she attempts to finish her collection by reading new letters from readers, seeking one question that might surprise and save her. As she reflects on the human condition with dark wit and philosophical exhaustion, an ominous knock at her door suggests an impending crisis.

title: Discourse on Wonders
author: Liz Duffy Adams
synopsis: Set in 1702, ten years after the Salem witch trials, this play explores what happens when Abigail Williams—one of the original accusers—returns to a coastal New England tavern seeking answers. When she questions whether witches actually exist and arrives just as the community is gripped by new fears of supernatural threats, the townspeople's old paranoia and desire for certainty reignite, leading them to accuse her of witchcraft and stage an impromptu trial.

title: Gidion's Knot
author: Johnna Adams
synopsis: A teacher and the mother of a deceased student meet for a parent-teacher conference that descends into a complex, emotionally charged conversation about a story the boy wrote that led to his suspension and eventual suicide. As the principal is delayed, the two women navigate blame, responsibility, art, and the unknowable truth of what drives a young person to darkness.

title: Nurture
author: Johanna Adams
synopsis: Two emotionally damaged single parents, Cheryl and Doug, meet at a children's dance recital and begin an increasingly dysfunctional relationship while their children forge an unsettling bond. As Doug and Cheryl move in together, they grapple with their incompetence as parents, their disturbing pasts, and a growing realization that their partnership may be based on mutual damage rather than genuine connection.

title: Or
author: Liz Duffy Adams
synopsis: Set in Restoration-era London (1666-1670), this witty comedy follows the newly freed Aphra Behn as she navigates her ambitions to become a playwright while entangled with the King, her former fellow spy William Scott, and the vivacious actress Nell Gwynne. As multiple visitors converge on her lodgings one fateful night, Aphra must juggle romantic entanglements, theatrical deadlines, and state secrets while discovering that freedom and love are far more complicated than she imagined.

title: Rainbow's End
author: Nic Adams
synopsis: In a copper Faraday cage conference room during an ancient coin auction lot viewing in Midtown East, a wealthy retiree named Ebenezer inspects rare coins with the help of Ciel, a struggling nonbinary temp attendant, and Bowen, an anxious junior operations manager. As the morning progresses, an intimate conversation develops between Ebenezer and Ciel, punctuated by their internal monologues—Ebenezer contemplating his mortality and generosity, and Ciel fantasizing about wealth and creative ambitions—while Bowen's farcical corporate enthusiasm and misguided attempts at inclusivity create comic tension.

title: Superstar!
author: Heather Adams
synopsis: Diana Davis, a barista who abandoned her dreams of medicine to care for her ailing mother and younger sister, meets Superstar/Espi, an alien superhero from the planet Pluton. When Espi's nemesis, the supervillain Malev Zertef, leaves her nearly dead, Diana saves her life and is recruited as her sidekick—an opportunity that could secure her mother's life-saving surgery. As Diana trains alongside Espi, both women must confront their pasts and the true meaning of heroism and sacrifice.
